reasoning: The primary statistical anchor provided by the time series tool is P(increase) = 0.39, reflecting a broader negative trend since September 2025 despite the recent late-November/early-December bounce. The stock has rallied sharply from ~$588 to $672 on the back of extremely positive news regarding 30% cuts to reality labs (metaverse) spending and more rationalized CapEx. While Wall Street is universally bullish on this cost turnaround—bringing strong immediate momentum—the $672 level represents a local peak after a very fast 15%+ run and could see near-term profit taking, aligning with the statistical model's lower base rate. Balancing the model's 0.39 bearish anchor with the extremely bullish fundamental catalyst and the instruction to stay close to 0.5, I set the short-term probability at 0.45. Forecasting forward, standard market drift and increasing uncertainty suggest probabilities should regress toward 0.5, pushing the 1-month and 3-month estimates to 0.47 and 0.49 respectively.
